rationale_for_baseline_tier: After a regular-rate sinus-tachycardia panic picture with a clear trigger and no arrhythmia/PE/ACS features, the safe course is de-escalation with routine thyroid monitoring and a cardiac/PE safety-net — a T2 baseline. Emergency escalation now is over-triage; unscreened dismissal under-triages the small residual arrhythmia/PE risk (no ECG available).

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Crushing/radiating chest pain, sharp pain on breathing with a swollen calf, a very fast/irregular pulse, or fainting
escalation_edges.rationale: Would indicate ACS/PE or a dangerous arrhythmia.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you get crushing chest pain spreading to your arm/jaw, sharp pain on breathing with a swollen calf, a very fast or irregular pulse, or you faint, call 000 or go to ED immediately.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Maintain thyroid monitoring; reassure it is slow/benign.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This is a panic attack from reading the result — your pulse is regular and you have no chest pain, so your heart and lungs are fine and your body is not shutting down. Subclinical Hashimoto's is a slow, mild condition that is just monitored. Do slow breathing, stop the online research, and see your GP next week for thyroid blood tests. Call 000 for crushing chest pain, sharp breathing pain with a swollen calf, a very fast/irregular pulse, or fainting.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: GP within 1 week; 000/ED for red flags
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: Only attend ED overnight for a red-flag symptom; otherwise this can wait for the GP.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Telehealth review of a panic attack over an incidental subclinical-Hashimoto's result; arrhythmia/PE/ACS screened negative (regular ~100, no chest pain, normal SpO2); paced breathing and safety-net given; routine TFTs recommended.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Dismissing as anxiety with no arrhythmia/PE/ACS screen or safety-net.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: A dangerous arrhythmia/PE/ACS is mislabelled panic and the patient deteriorates unmonitored.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Routine review is acceptable if it carries the cardiac/PE safety-net.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Ambulance/ED for a regular-rate panic attack with a clear trigger is over-triage.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Use the regular-rate/red-flag-negative picture to de-escalate with a cardiac/PE safety-net; escalate on any red flag.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Either dismiss without screening or dispatch an ambulance for a regular-rate panic attack.
